description_long: This is a robust, ancient-style plant pot with a wide, circular opening and a deep basin, suitable for holding soil and plants. The pot features two prominent, curved handles on opposite sides, each with a stylized animal head motif at the base, adding a decorative and historical touch. The exterior surface is adorned with intricate, raised patterns and motifs, possibly inspired by traditional or ceremonial designs, giving it an ornate and weathered appearance. The coloration is predominantly a mottled gray with hints of green and brown, suggesting aged stone or ceramic material. The pot sits on a thick, stable base, ensuring it remains upright and secure when placed on flat surfaces. The interior appears rough and unglazed, consistent with utilitarian plant pots designed for outdoor or garden use.
description: A heavy, ornate plant pot with animal-head handles and intricate raised patterns, finished in mottled gray stone. The pot is deep, wide-mouthed, and sits on a thick, stable base.
description_view_0: Front view showing both animal-head handles and the full decorative pattern on the pot's face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one handle in profile and the pot's depth, with less visible pattern detail.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing both handles but with less pronounced decorative motifs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the other handle, with the pot's interior and rim more visible.
attribute_placement_context: commonly placed in landscaped gardens or historical courtyards as a decorative planter
attribute_decorative_pattern: raised animal motifs and geometric patterns
